title: String literal with NUL escapes and > 512 source bytes decodes to wrong length/content
goal: Found by the P4 stress harness (2026-07-02, master 2692ca63). A string literal that contains at least one \x0; NUL escape AND is longer than 512 SOURCE bytes decodes to the wrong length (non-monotonic in the repeat count, so content is corrupted, not just truncated). Measured with unit ab\n\t\\\"\x41;\x0; (19 src -> 8 decoded chars): x26 (494 src) = 208 correct; x27 (513 src) = 217 WRONG; x28 = 215; x500 = 3915-3918 (expected 4000). Controls: NUL-free literals of 9.5k src decode exactly; \x0; x100 (400 src) exact; \x0; x300 (1200 src) = 287-297 WRONG. Likely the 512-byte-chunked literal intern/copy path using strlen-ish logic on NUL-bearing payloads (string-layer over-allocation audit family).
acceptance: parser_string_5k_escapes.esk extended with a >512-byte NUL literal keeps exact lengths
campaign_payoff: Binary payloads in literals (P6/PPM rendering, protocol fixtures) silently corrupt past 512 bytes.

verifications.evidence: string_nul_long_literal.esk still wrong AND path-divergent: -r prints 'OK 284', AOT prints 'OK 276' (expected 'OK 300'). NUL-bearing literal >512 source bytes decodes to wrong length, and JIT!=AOT. Stress XKNOWN r+aot.
